133.reason: [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly added, but tokenType is still set to AND, causing invalid token to be added to tokens list]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly added, but tokenType is still set to AND, causing invalid token to be added to tokens list]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly added, but the code still assigns tokenType to AND and proceeds to add a Token, which is incorrect—lexical errors should not produce tokens.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly added, but tokenType is still set to AND, which will cause an invalid token to be added to tokens list instead of skipping it]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly added, but tokenType is still set to AND, which will cause an invalid token to be added to tokens list instead of skipping it]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly added, but the code still assigns tokenType = TokenType.AND and proceeds to add a Token to the tokens list. This is incorrect: lexical errors should prevent token generation for that symbol.]

144.reason: [When a single '|' is encountered, an error is correctly added, but tokenType is still set to OR, causing invalid token to be added to tokens list]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '|' is encountered, an error is correctly added, but tokenType is still set to OR, causing invalid token to be added to tokens list]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: for a single '|', an error is recorded but the lexer still creates an OR token, which violates the specification that erroneous inputs should only output errors and no tokens.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: for a single '|', an error is recorded but tokenType is still set to OR, leading to incorrect token generation instead of skipping the erroneous symbol]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: for a single '|', an error is recorded but tokenType is still set to OR, leading to incorrect token generation]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: even when a single '|' triggers error 'a', the lexer still creates an OR token, which is wrong. After recording an error, no token should be generated for that invalid symbol.]
